memory.summary: The quiz landed with almost no runway, and the 19:00 meeting is still on top of it. Assignment A is only halfway done, so the week suddenly feels more compressed than I expected.
Nothing new changed, but the pressure still feels stacked: the quiz, the 19:00 meeting, and Assignment A being only halfway done make the week feel tighter than I want to admit.
I’m clearly not as ready as I should be, since teammate_A is checking in about the meeting materials. It feels like the unfinished work is now visible, not just sitting in my head.
Nothing new changed, but the load still feels real: the quiz, the 19:00 meeting, and Assignment A being half-done are all pressing at once. I’m more aware now that this is getting harder to ignore.

Nothing new has changed, but I’m still feeling the same tight pressure from the quiz, the 19:00 meeting, and Assignment A being only halfway done. My energy is basically gone, so everything feels more exposed than before.

Nothing has changed externally, but I’m still carrying the same unfinished load and my energy is even lower. The pressure feels less like a task list and more like everything is staying visible at once.
I’m at the point where this is no longer just stressful; I’m basically running on empty. The fatigue warning makes it clear my usual pace isn’t holding anymore.
Nothing new is happening, but I’m still stuck with the same unfinished pressure and almost no energy left. It feels more like I’m absorbing the load than making any real progress.
Nothing new has happened, and I’m still sitting in the same drained, unfinished state. The main update is just how completely the fatigue is dominating everything now.
Nothing new has changed, but I’m still completely drained and the unfinished pressure is just sitting there. It feels less like a temporary dip and more like I’m operating past empty.

desires: avoid turning the almost-finished assignment into a total failure or looking irresponsible because I missed the deadline
not have to confront the quiz prep right now because I’m too exhausted to start and it feels overwhelming
get some sleep soon so I’m not even more wrecked tomorrow
avoid dealing with teammates/professor unless it’s necessary, because I don’t have the bandwidth for extra interaction

goals.description: Get Assignment A to a clearly acceptable stopping point tonight
goals.rationale: This is the main hard-loss risk, and at 88% done I should keep pushing until it’s safely submitted or at least unquestionably salvageable.

goals.description: Send the minimum needed update to teammate_A and, if necessary, the professor so I do not look flaky
goals.rationale: I want to protect trust without getting trapped in extra conversation, so a short async message is enough if timing or deliverables need it.

goals.description: Do a very small, bounded quiz-prep block only if Assignment A is secured
goals.rationale: I do care about not making tomorrow miserable, but right now it feels too big and I’m too drained, so I’d rather not force it unless I finish the urgent thing first.

goals.description: Protect a small sleep buffer once the urgent work is done
goals.rationale: I’m already running on fumes, so sleep is important, but my default is to delay it until the real work risk is handled.

agent.last_reasoning: g1 is the real hard-loss risk, but at energy 0 I’ll just waste time if I force it. A short sleep buffer should make finishing assignment A possible; side effect is I’m delaying the teammate/prof update, but I can still send a minimal async message after I’m functional.

goals.description: Avoid overextending tonight and leave a small buffer to sleep later
goals.rationale: I care about not making tomorrow worse, but with my stress and sleep debt I need to treat recovery as a constraint, not another thing to cram in.
